title: Fix return statements in PyMuPDFScraper
body:
Hi, just a quick fix for two bugs in `PyMuPDFScraper`:

1. In case of problems, there were no `return` statements in the except blocks, that caused the function to implicitly return `None`, and that errors out [in here](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/master/gpt_researcher/scraper/scraper.py#L103C1-L113C22), because `None` can not be unpacked to three variables. I added returning of empty strings/lists, as is done in other scraper classes as well.

2. It was typed to return `str`, but actually it returns `tuple[str, list[str], str]`

files_modified.patch:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ def is_url(self) -> bool:
         except Exception:
             return False
 
-    def scrape(self) -> str:
+    def scrape(self) -> tuple[str, list[str], str]:
         """
         The `scrape` function uses PyMuPDFLoader to load a document from the provided link (either URL or local file)
         and returns the document as a string.
@@ -64,5 +64,7 @@ def scrape(self) -> str:
 
         except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
             print(f"Download timed out. Please check the link : {self.link}")
+            return "", [], ""
         except Exception as e:
             print(f"Error loading PDF : {self.link} {e}")
+            return "", [], ""

steps.operation: Fix `scrape` to always return a 3-tuple and correct its return type annotation
steps.reason: The scraper framework expects `scrape()` to return three values (text, list of links, title/metadata). Previously, the `except` blocks had no explicit return, causing an implicit `None` return and a runtime unpacking error in the caller. The function was also incorrectly annotated as returning `str` despite returning a tuple in normal operation.
steps.side_effects: Callers relying on the (incorrect) `str` type hint will now type-check correctly; error paths will no longer raise unpacking exceptions and will instead propagate empty values, which may change downstream behavior (e.g., components may treat an empty result as a successful scrape with no content unless they separately inspect logs/exceptions).

step_plan.summary: Ensures `PyMuPDFScraper.scrape()` consistently returns a `(str, list[str], str)` tuple by adding explicit empty-value returns in exception paths and correcting the function’s return type annotation.
